entries.name: airtable

This plugin makes Claude fluent in Airtable: creating bases and schema, working with records, and sharing UI for collaboration. Bundles the official Airtable MCP server.

Analyze observability data including log analytics with PPL and Query DSL, and distributed trace analysis with OpenTelemetry. Deploy to Amazon OpenSearch Service or OpenSearch Serverless with Bedrock integration for embeddings and RAG. Just ask your AI assistant to set up a search app, query logs, investigate traces, or deploy to AWS.

CodeRabbit provides external validation using a specialized AI architecture and 40+ integrated static analyzers—offering a different perspective that catches bugs, security vulnerabilities, logic errors, and edge cases. Context-aware analysis via AST parsing and codegraph relationships. Automatically incorporates CLAUDE.md and project coding guidelines into reviews. Useful after writing or modifying code, before commits, when implementing complex or security-sensitive logic, or when a second opinion would increase confidence in the changes. Returns specific findings with suggested fixes that can be applied immediately. Free to use.

entries.name: /best-of-n
entries.description: Added a new command /best-of-n that runs the same task in parallel across multiple models, each in its own isolated worktree, then compares outcomes.

entries.name: /plan
entries.description: Typing /plan takes you back to your current plan and its action menu. We've also added keyboard shortcuts in the prompt bar so you can use arrow keys to navigate options, Enter to execute the selected option, and Shift+Enter as a shortcut for "Build in cloud."

entries.name: /add-plugin
entries.description: Browse plugins at cursor.com/marketplace or install directly in the editor with /add-plugin.

entries.name: /resume
entries.description: /resume now sorts by last interaction time, not creation time, so your most recent conversations appear first.

entries.name: /auto-run
entries.description: /auto-run, /max-mode, /vim, and similar commands now toggle with a single invocation. Current status is shown in the command description.

entries.name: /max-mode
entries.description: /auto-run, /max-mode, /vim, and similar commands now toggle with a single invocation. Current status is shown in the command description.

entries.name: /vim
entries.description: /auto-run, /max-mode, /vim, and similar commands now toggle with a single invocation. Current status is shown in the command description.

entries.name: batch
entries.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5\u201330 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

entries.name: claude-in-chrome
entries.description: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension).

entries.name: debug
entries.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

entries.name: keybindings-help
entries.description: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

entries.name: simplify
entries.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.

entries.name: update-config
entries.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

entries.name: dream
entries.description: Reflective memory consolidation \u2014 review recent activity, synthesize learnings into typed memory files, and prune stale entries.

entries.name: loop
entries.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.

entries.name: schedule
entries.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.
